cases.description: FixtureSplitPlan over FixtureFileContent produces two new files with exact section-range content and distinct allocated fileIDs
Each new file's on-disk content, read back via cs.Read semantics-equivalent os.ReadFile(cs.ContentPath), equals exactly originalContent[Start:End] concatenated in range order
Allocated fileIDs are distinct from each other and from any previously-allocated fileID (idAlloc.Next() monotonicity)
A SplitFileProposal with multiple non-contiguous SectionRanges is assembled in order
A zero-length SectionRange (Start == End) is accepted and contributes no bytes
A SectionRange with End > len(originalContent) is rejected with a clear error, no partial writes/allocations
A SectionRange with Start > End is rejected with a clear error
Two SplitFileProposals whose SectionRanges overlap are rejected with a clear error, no partial writes/allocations
Two SplitFileProposals sharing the same NewPath are rejected with a clear error
A SplitPlan with zero Files is rejected with a clear error
Executing the plan does not create any catalog.CatalogRecord for the new fileIDs (cat.Get on the new fileID still returns ErrNotFound afterward)
All prior engine/split subtasks (2b.1.1-2b.1.3, 2b.2.1-2b.2.2) still pass with -race
engine/catalog package tests unaffected by reuse of ContentPath/IDAllocator
